Clone the TensorFlow models repository from https://github.com/tensorflow/models.git
Ensure Python 3.8+ environment with TensorFlow 2.12 installed via pip (e.g., pip install tensorflow==2.12)
Navigate to the 'docs/vision' directory inside the cloned repository
Open the 'object_detection.ipynb' notebook in a Jupyter or Colab environment
Run all notebook cells sequentially up to the cell labeled 'Import required libraries from tensorflow models as image below', which attempts to import the object_detection module and its dependencies
Observe the import error or failure in this step, typically due to missing or misconfigured dependencies in the object_detection library

Specifically, verify that the import failure occurs when the notebook executes lines similar to 'from object_detection.utils import config_util' or 'from object_detection.utils import visualization_utils as vis_util'

Prepare a minimal test Python script to reproduce the import failure outside the notebook context by attempting 'import object_detection.utils' after setting PYTHONPATH to the root of the cloned models repository
In the test script, assert that executing 'import object_detection.utils' raises an ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError
Document the exact error message text and stack trace produced during the import failure
Verify that required protobuf files are compiled and that the PYTHONPATH environment variable includes the 'models/research' and 'models/research/slim' directories as per the setup instructions

Attempt to run 'python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt' inside the 'models/research' directory to ensure dependencies are installed, then rerun the notebook import cell to confirm the bug reproduces
Capture logs or error outputs from the notebook or script that confirm the import failure persists despite the above steps
Conclude the reproduction by asserting that the notebook import cell fails with the captured ImportError, preventing further execution
